root_text.sn56.35:1.3: ‘ehambho purisa, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ taṁ sattisatena hanissanti, majjhanhikasamayaṁ sattisatena hanissanti, sāyanhasamayaṁ sattisatena hanissanti.
root_text.sn56.35:1.4: So kho tvaṁ, ambho purisa, divase divase tīhi tīhi sattisatehi haññamāno vassasatāyuko vassasatajīvī vassasatassa accayena anabhisametāni cattāri ariyasaccāni abhisamessasī’ti.
root_text.sn56.35:2.4: pubbā koṭi nappaññāyati sattippahārānaṁ asippahārānaṁ usuppahārānaṁ pharasuppahārānaṁ.
root_text.sn56.35:2.6: Na kho panāhaṁ, bhikkhave, saha dukkhena, saha domanassena catunnaṁ ariyasaccānaṁ abhisamayaṁ vadāmi;
root_text.sn56.35:2.7: api cāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sahāva sukhena, sahāva somanassena catunnaṁ ariyasaccānaṁ abhisamayaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.sn56.35:3.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.35:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose there was a man with a lifespan of a hundred years.
translation_text.sn56.35:1.2: And someone might say to him:
translation_text.sn56.35:1.3: ‘Come now, my friend, they’ll strike you with a hundred spears in the morning, at midday, and in the late afternoon.
translation_text.sn56.35:1.4: And you’ll live for a hundred years being struck with three hundred spears every day. But when a hundred years have passed, you will comprehend the four noble truths for the first time.’
translation_text.sn56.35:2.1: For an earnest gentleman this is sufficient reason to submit.
translation_text.sn56.35:2.3: This transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn56.35:2.4: No first point is found of blows by spears, swords, arrows, and axes.
translation_text.sn56.35:2.6: But the comprehension of the four noble truths doesn’t come with pain or sadness, I say.
translation_text.sn56.35:2.7: Rather, the comprehension of the four noble truths comes only with pleasure and happiness, I say.
translation_text.sn56.35:2.9: The noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.35:3.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”

variant_text.sn56.35:2.4: asippahārānaṁ usuppahārānaṁ pharasuppahārānaṁ → asippahārānaṁ (bj), asipahārānam parasupahārānaṁ (pts1ed), asippahārānaṁ pharasuppahārānaṁ (mr)
